title: EAFIX SSOT Registry Delivery Plan-Obligation Coverage Matrix
authority_notice: Generated, non-authoritative artifact. Do not hand-edit. Regenerate from the bound target plan and canonical verification specification.
operating_rule: Every target-plan obligation must map to one or more verification checks. A pass verdict requires fresh evidence at the bound target commit. This matrix defines coverage only; it does not claim execution or delivery.

source_value_snapshot.purpose: Establish one active plan, resolve current authority and repository constraints, verify all source hashes, and freeze the read-only baseline.

tasks.title: Verify source-plan hashes and relationships
actions: Hash every uploaded source and record canonical filename, uploaded filename, document ID, version, byte size, and SHA-256.
Confirm the two naming plans are byte-identical.
Confirm remediation v1.1.0 supersedes v1.0.0 and incorporates amendment v1.0.1.
Confirm the parity plan and completion roadmap are now present and remove their former missing-source status.
Record every source as absorbed, superseded, duplicate, provenance-only, or reference-only.
acceptance: All 13 source entries are hashable.
There are 12 unique content hashes because the naming plans are byte-identical.
No source plan remains an active sequencing dependency after approval.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop the task and preserve evidence if acceptance cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Resolve mandatory routing references
actions: Read eafix_project_knowledge_reference_routing_instructions.json first.
Resolve every required and companion document against current master.

Read current doc_authority.json, module_catalog.json, process_step_catalog.json, updated_trading_process_aligned.json, file_module_mapping.csv, eafix_services_ai_reference_20260510.json, module_naming_governance.json, registry_index.json, and any routed subject authority used by a task.
Record stale, moved, missing, duplicated, or conflicting authority paths.
Apply the routing file's conflict resolution before accepting any source-plan claim.
acceptance: Every routed reference has resolved, missing, stale, or needs-review status.
No missing document is silently replaced.
The active naming-governance path is resolved before module naming decisions.
Current authority files remain authoritative until their governed cutover.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop the task and preserve evidence if acceptance cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Resolve branch, ruleset, and CI execution constraints
actions: Resolve the repository default branch, allowed branch namespaces, required checks, CODEOWNERS constraints, and current rulesets from live repository state.
Check whether GitHub Actions jobs can start and distinguish code failure from account or billing startup blockage.
Define a branch name per bounded pull request only after current rules are known.
Record the current master commit SHA used for every baseline and evidence capture.
acceptance: No source plan's literal branch name overrides current repository rules.
No direct push to master is permitted.
Skipped, neutral, or startup-blocked checks are not counted as passing.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop the task and preserve evidence if acceptance cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Capture current registry tooling behavior
actions: Capture baseline behavior.
acceptance: Every action and source-derived requirement in this task is completed.
All declared outputs and required evidence are produced or explicitly recorded as not applicable with approved rationale.
No fail-closed condition is triggered and the task leaves the repository within its approved write scope.
evidence_required: Whether two normal builds modify registry_build_manifest.json
tasks.fail_closed: Stop this task and preserve evidence if any acceptance condition cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Probe module-to-process foreign-reference baseline
actions: Run a read-only baseline probe of every module process_step_ids value against process registry step_id values before adding enforcement.
Use an inline Python command or an untracked temporary script.
Parse both JSONL files without importing the validator being changed.
Build the set of process step_id values.
Emit one result for every module process_step_id.
Write the probe result outside the repository or to an ignored temporary path.
acceptance: The probe performs no repository writes.
The result selects exactly one execution profile through GATE-REG-FK-BASELINE.
Invalid references are discovered before validator or acceptance-gate changes are implemented.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop this task and preserve evidence if any acceptance condition cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Freeze the current authority, registry, process, and repository baselines
preconditions: Working tree is clean and current master SHA is recorded.
actions: Measure authored registry counts, generated output inventory, schema versions, current pointers, authority states, conflict queue size, needs_review counts, orphan counts, parity-tool presence, and process-document inventory.
Measure current module universe counts in every relevant authority and proposed source rather than copying 27, 28, 34, or 291 as permanent constants.
Inventory every tracked repository path, symlink, submodule, generated path, runtime-only path, and top-level root.
Capture current authority file hashes and current process/source document hashes.
Select exactly one clean or dirty foreign-key execution profile.
acceptance: Every count is computed from committed files.
The working tree returns to clean state.
Exactly one execution profile is selected.
All later acceptance constants are linked to governed expected-count or baseline files rather than copied literals.
tasks.fail_closed: If the repository cannot be baselined reproducibly, stop all implementation work.

tasks.title: Ratify this final merged plan as the sole sequencing authority
actions: Review this plan against the source consolidation record and resolved-conflict register.
Record approval in the decision registry or approved governance location.
Classify all predecessor implementation plans as absorbed and superseded for sequencing while retaining their hashes and source task IDs as provenance.
Do not mark current subject authorities superseded merely because this implementation plan is approved.
acceptance: Exactly one active implementation-sequencing plan remains.
All source-plan requirements remain traceable by source document and source task ID.
The final plan itself does not become subject-matter authority for module, process, contract, MT4, calendar, UI, or file-mapping facts.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop the task and preserve evidence if acceptance cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Create bounded pull-request and write-scope execution ledger
preconditions: The final merged plan is approved.
actions: Create one ledger record per planned PR with phase, task IDs, allowed paths, forbidden paths, branch, owner, expected evidence, rollback, and gate.
Split large burn-down and migration phases into bounded PR batches under the stricter source discipline.
Require every implementation PR to reference the final plan ID and exact task IDs.
acceptance: Every task belongs to an explicit write scope.
No PR mixes tooling and authored-data correction unless this plan explicitly permits it.
No PR mixes authority cutover with physical migration.
tasks.fail_closed: Do not create an implementation branch without a ledger entry.

pass_condition: Final plan approved as sole sequencing authority
All routed references resolved or explicitly blocked
Registry, process, repository, and authority baselines frozen
Exactly one FK execution profile selected
exit_gate.on_fail: Stop. No registry, authority, or migration implementation may begin.

tasks.title: Run the full candidate-integrity validation matrix
preconditions: The selected foreign-key profile is recorded.
actions: Run every remediation v1.1.0 validation command.
Use registry_expected_counts.json or another governed committed expected-count file rather than a hardcoded record total.
If the dirty FK profile was selected, do not claim phase completion until the separate data correction and enforcement tasks both pass.

foreign_reference_errors.PROFILE-DIRTY-FK-BASELINE: Zero after the linked data correction and enforcement track complete; until then, the unresolved baseline must be explicitly reported and the complete plan remains incomplete.

required_results.authority_parity_mismatch_count: advisory; record exact count and classifications

acceptance: Python 3.9 and Python 3.11 pass.
Two normal builds produce no diff.
--check writes no files.
No authority cutover occurs.
evidence_required: Read-only FK baseline probe and selected execution profile
Authority-parity report source inventory and classification counts
Proof that parity mismatches did not change authority
Tamper each output class and prove --check fails without rewriting.
Inject an invalid module process_step_id in a temporary fixture and prove reference validation fails.
Restore clean state and prove all validation passes.
Alter an authority fixture value and prove the comparator emits conflicting_value without modifying either source.
Remove a required authority fixture and prove the comparator reports an unavailable source or execution error according to the rule.
Run the parity comparator twice and prove byte-stable output.
tasks.fail_closed: Any structural, deterministic, compatibility, or unexplained count failure blocks the phase.

tasks.title: Publish and merge the bounded registry-engine remediation PR
preconditions: All local validation passes and CI can execute.
actions: Create a branch permitted by current rules.
Commit only registry engine, tests, CI, and generated outputs within the approved scope.
Open a PR with defect, test, determinism, generated-diff, and governance-boundary evidence.
Merge only after every required check executes and passes.

acceptance.requirement: All registry counts exactly match registry_expected_counts.json; any intentional change is separately approved and evidence-linked.
Schema, ID, enabled foreign-reference, and authority errors are zero.
The module-to-process read-only probe runs before blocking validator enforcement and selects exactly one execution profile.
A dirty FK baseline creates a separate governed data-defect record; no value is guessed, deleted, or silently rewritten.
Every managed candidate generated artifact is checked by --check.
--check writes no files.
The manifest contains no self-hash and all listed hashes verify.
Manifest paths use forward slashes and are byte-stable across supported platforms.
Blocking module process_step_ids validation is enabled only against a clean baseline and then reports zero errors.
Candidate-integrity tests pass on Python 3.9 and 3.11.
Two consecutive candidate builds produce no Git diff.
Tampering any managed candidate output causes --check to fail without rewriting it.
Registry Candidate Integrity is a blocking CI job.
Registry Authority Parity (Shadow) compares candidates with routed current authorities and emits a schema-valid deterministic report.
Authority-parity mismatch counts are advisory in v1.1.0, while comparator execution and report validity are mandatory.
Every parity comparison cites an authority document ID and path or is explicitly classified not_comparable/unavailable.
The 25 review items and two orphan items remain visible unless separately resolved with evidence.
No routing, document-authority, supersession, or registry authority cutover occurs.
GitHub checks execute after the account lock is removed; startup-blocked checks are not treated as passing.
Doc ID Validation, PR title lint, and all repository-required checks pass.

evidence_required: Explicit confirmation that authority cutover was not performed
SHA-256 of the v1.0.0 base plan
FK baseline probe artifact and selected execution profile
Linked data-defect issue/PR evidence when PROFILE-DIRTY-FK-BASELINE is selected
Proof that comparator execution did not mutate candidate or authority sources
tasks.fail_closed: Do not merge with startup-blocked CI, unexplained generated diffs, authority changes, or deferred blocking validation.

pass_condition: Enabled foreign-reference errors = 0 under selected profile
exit_gate.on_fail: Stop authority-parity and data-correction work; repair the registry engine only.

source_value_snapshot.title: Run the full candidate-integrity validation matrix
preconditions: The selected foreign-key profile is recorded.
actions: Run every remediation v1.1.0 validation command.
Use registry_expected_counts.json or another governed committed expected-count file rather than a hardcoded record total.
If the dirty FK profile was selected, do not claim phase completion until the separate data correction and enforcement tasks both pass.

foreign_reference_errors.PROFILE-DIRTY-FK-BASELINE: Zero after the linked data correction and enforcement track complete; until then, the unresolved baseline must be explicitly reported and the complete plan remains incomplete.

required_results.authority_parity_mismatch_count: advisory; record exact count and classifications

acceptance: Python 3.9 and Python 3.11 pass.
Two normal builds produce no diff.
--check writes no files.
No authority cutover occurs.
evidence_required: Read-only FK baseline probe and selected execution profile
Authority-parity report source inventory and classification counts
Proof that parity mismatches did not change authority
Tamper each output class and prove --check fails without rewriting.
Inject an invalid module process_step_id in a temporary fixture and prove reference validation fails.
Restore clean state and prove all validation passes.
Alter an authority fixture value and prove the comparator emits conflicting_value without modifying either source.
Remove a required authority fixture and prove the comparator reports an unavailable source or execution error according to the rule.
Run the parity comparator twice and prove byte-stable output.

obligations.requirement: Verify task SSOT-0122 (Publish and merge the bounded registry-engine remediation PR) satisfied preconditions, dependencies, actions, write scope, outputs, acceptance, evidence, human-gate, and completion-record requirements.

source_value_snapshot.title: Publish and merge the bounded registry-engine remediation PR
preconditions: All local validation passes and CI can execute.
actions: Create a branch permitted by current rules.
Commit only registry engine, tests, CI, and generated outputs within the approved scope.
Open a PR with defect, test, determinism, generated-diff, and governance-boundary evidence.
Merge only after every required check executes and passes.

acceptance.requirement: All registry counts exactly match registry_expected_counts.json; any intentional change is separately approved and evidence-linked.
Schema, ID, enabled foreign-reference, and authority errors are zero.
The module-to-process read-only probe runs before blocking validator enforcement and selects exactly one execution profile.
A dirty FK baseline creates a separate governed data-defect record; no value is guessed, deleted, or silently rewritten.
Every managed candidate generated artifact is checked by --check.
--check writes no files.
The manifest contains no self-hash and all listed hashes verify.
Manifest paths use forward slashes and are byte-stable across supported platforms.
Blocking module process_step_ids validation is enabled only against a clean baseline and then reports zero errors.
Candidate-integrity tests pass on Python 3.9 and 3.11.
Two consecutive candidate builds produce no Git diff.
Tampering any managed candidate output causes --check to fail without rewriting it.
Registry Candidate Integrity is a blocking CI job.
Registry Authority Parity (Shadow) compares candidates with routed current authorities and emits a schema-valid deterministic report.
Authority-parity mismatch counts are advisory in v1.1.0, while comparator execution and report validity are mandatory.
Every parity comparison cites an authority document ID and path or is explicitly classified not_comparable/unavailable.
The 25 review items and two orphan items remain visible unless separately resolved with evidence.
No routing, document-authority, supersession, or registry authority cutover occurs.
GitHub checks execute after the account lock is removed; startup-blocked checks are not treated as passing.
Doc ID Validation, PR title lint, and all repository-required checks pass.

evidence_required: Explicit confirmation that authority cutover was not performed
SHA-256 of the v1.0.0 base plan
FK baseline probe artifact and selected execution profile
Linked data-defect issue/PR evidence when PROFILE-DIRTY-FK-BASELINE is selected
Proof that comparator execution did not mutate candidate or authority sources

source_value_snapshot.purpose: Project current authorities deterministically, compare them to authored registries, emit stable parity records, freeze known divergences, and make regressions blocking without changing authority.

tasks.title: Add a JSON Schema for the parity authority map under 01_canonical_registries/schemas/
actions: Add a JSON Schema for the parity authority map under 01_canonical_registries/schemas/.
coverage is an enum: authority_covered, no_declared_authority, authority_unavailable.
authority_covered requires authority_path, join_key, and non-empty compared_fields.
no_declared_authority forbids authority_path and requires a reason.
Schema validation of this file is a blocking error class.

tasks.title: Implement tools/registries/authority_projection.py with one pure adapter per authority document format
actions: Implement tools/registries/authority_projection.py with one pure adapter per authority document format.
Adapters are pure functions: document bytes in, normalized fact mapping out.
Adapters never write, never mutate input, and never reach across to the registries.
Every adapter declares which authority fields it projects AND which it deliberately does not.
An authority field that is projected by no adapter and has no registry counterpart must surface as unrepresentable, not vanish.
CSV parsing is explicit about encoding, delimiter, and header handling. No dialect sniffing: sniffing is non-deterministic across inputs.
project_module_catalog (JSON catalog -> module_id facts)
project_process_step_catalog (JSON catalog -> process_step_id facts)
project_aligned_trading_process (JSON -> phase_id and contract_id facts)
project_file_module_mapping (CSV -> file_path facts)
acceptance: Running an adapter twice on identical bytes yields identical output.
Adapter output ordering is deterministic (sorted by join key).
No adapter raises on an unexpected extra field; it records it as unprojected.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop this task and preserve evidence if any acceptance condition cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Implement the matching registry-side projection into the same normalized field vocabulary
actions: Implement the matching registry-side projection into the same normalized field vocabulary.
Field vocabulary is declared once and shared. Two spellings of the same concept across the two sides is a defect.
Registry projection reads only the authored JSONL, never the generated snapshots. Comparing generated output to an authority would test the renderer, not the data.

actions: Render the Markdown parity view for human review.
Grouped by registry, then by verdict severity: field_divergence, authority_only, registry_only, unrepresentable, not_comparable.
Every divergence row shows parity_id, join key, field, registry value, authority value, disposition.

tasks.title: Generate the initial baseline from the first clean parity run, then commit it as authored data
actions: Generate the initial baseline from the first clean parity run, then commit it as authored data.
registry_authority_parity_baseline.json contains every current non-agree parity_id with verdict and disposition unreviewed.
The baseline is authored and governed. After this initial generation it is never regenerated wholesale; it is edited deliberately, record by record.
The baseline stores parity_id and verdict only, not values. A divergence whose values change but whose identity persists stays baselined; that is intended.
acceptance: A parity run against the frozen baseline reports new_divergences = 0.
The baseline record count equals the current non-agree parity record count.

tasks.title: Implement evaluate_baseline with a one-way ratchet
actions: Implement evaluate_baseline with a one-way ratchet.
new_divergence (parity_id not in baseline) is BLOCKING.
known_divergence (parity_id in baseline) is advisory.
resolved_divergence (baseline parity_id no longer divergent) is advisory but MUST be reported loudly and prompts baseline tightening.
Adding an entry to the baseline is a reviewable governance change and must be visible in the PR diff. Never allow tooling to append to the baseline automatically.

tasks.title: Implement the complete authority-parity regression and mutation suite
actions: Implement every required parity test.
Use hand-verifiable fixtures and prove parity writes no authored data.
Run on Python 3.9 and 3.11 and on Windows/Linux-compatible paths.
acceptance: test_authority_map_validates_against_doc_authority
test_every_registry_has_explicit_coverage_classification
test_adapters_are_pure_and_deterministic
test_adapter_output_is_stably_sorted
test_missing_authority_yields_authority_unavailable_not_agree
test_registry_only_detected
test_authority_only_detected
test_field_divergence_detected
test_unrepresentable_authority_field_is_reported
test_not_comparable_is_never_counted_as_agree
test_parity_id_is_stable_across_runs
test_parity_id_is_stable_when_values_change
test_parity_id_changes_when_field_or_join_key_changes
test_new_divergence_is_blocking
test_known_divergence_is_advisory
test_resolved_divergence_is_reported
test_stale_baseline_entry_is_blocking
test_report_only_does_not_hide_new_divergence
test_parity_report_is_byte_stable_across_two_builds
test_check_detects_parity_report_drift_without_writing
test_check_detects_parity_markdown_drift_without_writing
test_parity_engine_writes_no_authored_files
test_parity_engine_reads_authored_jsonl_not_generated_snapshots
test_current_dataset_produces_zero_new_divergences_against_baseline
tasks.fail_closed: Any missing verdict, baseline, authority-unavailable, or no-write test blocks parity completion.

test_rules: Use temporary directories, synthetic fixtures, or patched roots. Never assert on live production counts other than in the single dataset test.
Fixture authorities and fixture registries must be small and hand-verifiable.
Never leave committed files modified after a test.
Tests must be order-independent and pass on Windows and Linux.
Tests must pass on Python 3.9 and 3.11.

tasks.title: Run the full parity validation matrix and freeze the honest baseline
preconditions: Candidate-integrity remediation is merged and deterministic.
actions: Run every parity validation command and mutation test.
Verify all ten registries have explicit coverage classifications.
Verify the baseline contains every current non-agree parity_id and that new_divergences and stale_baseline_entries are zero.
Treat a large known-divergence baseline as an honest deliverable, not a failure.
Confirm zero authored registry, routing, doc-authority, or registry-index changes.

acceptance.known_divergences: Any value. This number is expected to be large, especially for contract_registry. A low number here would be more suspicious than a high one, and would suggest the adapters are not actually comparing anything.
acceptance.not_comparable: Any value. Must be visible, not zero.
acceptance.unrepresentable: Any value. Must be visible, not zero.

evidence_required: Proof that parity execution modified zero authored files
Tamper the parity report and prove --check fails without rewriting it.
Inject a synthetic divergence into a fixture registry and prove it is reported as new_divergence and blocks.
Remove a fixture authority file and prove the result is authority_unavailable, not agree.
Delete a baseline entry that still diverges and prove the run blocks.
Add a baseline entry for a non-existent parity_id and prove stale-entry detection blocks.
Restore clean state and prove new_divergences returns to zero.
tasks.fail_closed: Any missing coverage classification, unstable parity_id, new divergence, stale baseline entry, unavailable required authority, or authored-data mutation blocks the phase.

tasks.title: Publish and merge the bounded authority-parity PR
preconditions: Local parity validation passes and CI can execute.
actions: Use a branch permitted by current repository rules.
Open a PR containing only parity tooling, tests, governed map/baseline files, CI changes, and generated parity outputs.
Open one tracking issue per registry with a non-zero baseline.
Merge only after structural and parity jobs execute and pass on Python 3.9 and 3.11.
acceptance: parity_authority_map.json exists, is schema-valid, and cross-validates against doc_authority.json.
All 10 registries carry an explicit coverage classification; none is silently omitted.
registry_authority_parity_report.json is a managed, deterministic, manifest-hashed, --check-covered generated artifact.
authority_parity.generated.md renders a human-reviewable divergence view with deterministic ordering.
Every compared fact carries a closed-taxonomy verdict and a stable parity_id.
not_comparable and unrepresentable are counted separately from agree and are never aggregated into a pass rate.
A missing authority yields authority_unavailable and never degrades to agree.
registry_authority_parity_baseline.json is frozen and committed as authored, reviewable data.
New divergences block CI. Known divergences are advisory. Resolved divergences are reported.
Stale baseline entries block CI.
The parity engine writes zero authored files and reads authored JSONL rather than generated snapshots.
Two consecutive builds produce byte-identical parity output.
The suite passes on Python 3.9 and 3.11.
Zero authored registry records changed.
Zero authority, routing, doc_authority, or registry_index changes.
The 25 review items and two orphan classifications remain visible.
No authority cutover occurs.
Machine-checkable cutover criteria are documented and not executed.
evidence_required: Authority inventory table with SHA-256 per authority document
Two-build no-diff proof for parity artifacts
Mutation-test output for all six mutation cases
Proof that zero authored JSONL files were modified
Explicit confirmation that authority cutover was not performed
Explicit confirmation that no divergence was resolved by editing a registry record
tasks.fail_closed: Do not merge if the PR resolves divergences, edits authority, changes candidate status, or hides known baseline findings.

pass_condition: All 10 registries have explicit coverage classification
Parity authority map cross-validates against current doc authority
Stable parity IDs and closed verdict taxonomy validated
Frozen baseline matches current non-agree facts
Zero authored data or authority mutations
exit_gate.on_fail: Stop. Do not begin burn-down using untrusted parity output.

source_value_snapshot.title: Implement tools/registries/authority_projection.py with one pure adapter per authority document format
actions: Implement tools/registries/authority_projection.py with one pure adapter per authority document format.
Adapters are pure functions: document bytes in, normalized fact mapping out.
Adapters never write, never mutate input, and never reach across to the registries.
Every adapter declares which authority fields it projects AND which it deliberately does not.
An authority field that is projected by no adapter and has no registry counterpart must surface as unrepresentable, not vanish.
CSV parsing is explicit about encoding, delimiter, and header handling. No dialect sniffing: sniffing is non-deterministic across inputs.

project_process_step_catalog (JSON catalog -> process_step_id facts)
project_aligned_trading_process (JSON -> phase_id and contract_id facts)
project_file_module_mapping (CSV -> file_path facts)

source_value_snapshot.title: Implement evaluate_baseline with a one-way ratchet
actions: Implement evaluate_baseline with a one-way ratchet.
new_divergence (parity_id not in baseline) is BLOCKING.
known_divergence (parity_id in baseline) is advisory.
resolved_divergence (baseline parity_id no longer divergent) is advisory but MUST be reported loudly and prompts baseline tightening.
Adding an entry to the baseline is a reviewable governance change and must be visible in the PR diff. Never allow tooling to append to the baseline automatically.

Never leave committed files modified after a test.
Tests must be order-independent and pass on Windows and Linux.
Tests must pass on Python 3.9 and 3.11.

source_value_snapshot.title: Run the full parity validation matrix and freeze the honest baseline
preconditions: Candidate-integrity remediation is merged and deterministic.
actions: Run every parity validation command and mutation test.
Verify all ten registries have explicit coverage classifications.
Verify the baseline contains every current non-agree parity_id and that new_divergences and stale_baseline_entries are zero.
Treat a large known-divergence baseline as an honest deliverable, not a failure.
Confirm zero authored registry, routing, doc-authority, or registry-index changes.

acceptance.known_divergences: Any value. This number is expected to be large, especially for contract_registry. A low number here would be more suspicious than a high one, and would suggest the adapters are not actually comparing anything.
acceptance.not_comparable: Any value. Must be visible, not zero.
acceptance.unrepresentable: Any value. Must be visible, not zero.

evidence_required: Proof that parity execution modified zero authored files
Tamper the parity report and prove --check fails without rewriting it.
Inject a synthetic divergence into a fixture registry and prove it is reported as new_divergence and blocks.
Remove a fixture authority file and prove the result is authority_unavailable, not agree.
Delete a baseline entry that still diverges and prove the run blocks.
Add a baseline entry for a non-existent parity_id and prove stale-entry detection blocks.
Restore clean state and prove new_divergences returns to zero.
source_value_snapshot.fail_closed: Any missing coverage classification, unstable parity_id, new divergence, stale baseline entry, unavailable required authority, or authored-data mutation blocks the phase.

source_value_snapshot.title: Publish and merge the bounded authority-parity PR
preconditions: Local parity validation passes and CI can execute.
actions: Use a branch permitted by current repository rules.
Open a PR containing only parity tooling, tests, governed map/baseline files, CI changes, and generated parity outputs.
Open one tracking issue per registry with a non-zero baseline.
Merge only after structural and parity jobs execute and pass on Python 3.9 and 3.11.
acceptance: parity_authority_map.json exists, is schema-valid, and cross-validates against doc_authority.json.
All 10 registries carry an explicit coverage classification; none is silently omitted.
registry_authority_parity_report.json is a managed, deterministic, manifest-hashed, --check-covered generated artifact.
authority_parity.generated.md renders a human-reviewable divergence view with deterministic ordering.
Every compared fact carries a closed-taxonomy verdict and a stable parity_id.
not_comparable and unrepresentable are counted separately from agree and are never aggregated into a pass rate.
A missing authority yields authority_unavailable and never degrades to agree.
registry_authority_parity_baseline.json is frozen and committed as authored, reviewable data.
New divergences block CI. Known divergences are advisory. Resolved divergences are reported.
Stale baseline entries block CI.
The parity engine writes zero authored files and reads authored JSONL rather than generated snapshots.
Two consecutive builds produce byte-identical parity output.
The suite passes on Python 3.9 and 3.11.
Zero authored registry records changed.
Zero authority, routing, doc_authority, or registry_index changes.
The 25 review items and two orphan classifications remain visible.
No authority cutover occurs.
Machine-checkable cutover criteria are documented and not executed.
evidence_required: Authority inventory table with SHA-256 per authority document
Two-build no-diff proof for parity artifacts
Mutation-test output for all six mutation cases
Proof that zero authored JSONL files were modified
Explicit confirmation that authority cutover was not performed
Explicit confirmation that no divergence was resolved by editing a registry record

obligations.requirement: Verify phase PHASE-03 (Define field-level SSOT authority and registry governance) executed after its dependencies, all tasks were covered, and its exit gate controlled progression.

source_value_snapshot.name: Define field-level SSOT authority and registry governance
source_value_snapshot.purpose: Create the authority manifest, authored/generated model, common record lifecycle, artifact classifications, authority bases, and per-registry cutover-state contract.

tasks.title: Approve authored-versus-generated registry model
actions: Record HD-SSOT-002.
Declare JSONL registries authored canonical candidates and *.current.json generated projections.
Define current-pointer and compatibility-projection behavior.
outputs: decision registry record for authored/generated model
acceptance: Exactly one authored source exists for each registry.
Generated views are non-editable.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop the task and preserve evidence if acceptance cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Create registry authority manifest schema
actions: Define field groups, canonical source, source type, owner, schema, current pointer, cutover state, external authority refs, projections, and conflict policy.
Require no overlapping active ownership.
acceptance: Schema rejects duplicate active owners for the same fact group.
Schema supports registered external authorities without duplicating their content.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop the task and preserve evidence if acceptance cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Create candidate registry authority manifest
actions: Map all governed fact groups to candidate registries or registered specialized authorities.
Mark every mapping pre_cutover.
Register contract schemas, MT4 reference, UI catalog, calendar DES rules, and other retained specialized authorities in artifact registry.
acceptance: Every governed fact group has exactly one proposed owner.
Every external authority is registered and hash-addressable.
No cutover state is active yet.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop the task and preserve evidence if acceptance cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Publish common registry record and lifecycle policy
actions: Define required common fields, statuses, stable ID reuse prohibition, supersession, provenance, valid-time, decision references, and content hashing.
Define change classes and approval levels.
acceptance: All ten registry schemas compose with the common contract.
Lifecycle transitions are validated.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop the task and preserve evidence if acceptance cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Define standalone-artifact classification
actions: Classify ratification files, crosswalks, inventories, policies, trees, and migration reports as canonical registry records, immutable approval evidence, or generated projections.
Prohibit independently editable duplicate authority files.
acceptance: Every planned JSON artifact has one classification.
No planned artifact can silently become a second SSOT.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop the task and preserve evidence if acceptance cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Approve field-level authority model
actions: Record HD-SSOT-003.
Review coverage matrix and specialized-authority boundaries.
Approve cutover criteria and rollback semantics.
acceptance: Authority model is human-approved.
No unresolved overlapping field ownership remains.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop the task and preserve evidence if acceptance cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Define per-registry authority-basis and cutover-state contracts
preconditions: Parity coverage classifications exist for all ten registries.
actions: For each registry, declare one authority basis: predecessor parity authority, born-canonical with substitute evidence validators, or governed retirement.
Represent authority status per registry rather than as one global flip.
Define pre_cutover, stability_observation, canonical, superseded, retired, and rollback states.
Require a governance decision and evidence bundle for every state transition.
acceptance: All ten registries have exactly one proposed authority basis.
No no_declared_authority registry is treated as verified merely because it lacks a comparator.
No global cutover can bypass per-registry criteria.
tasks.fail_closed: Any registry without an authority basis blocks later cutover.

tasks.title: Define immutable evidence, decision, projection, and active-authority classifications
actions: Classify every planned standalone artifact as active authority, registry record, immutable approval evidence, generated projection, compatibility projection, operational procedure, or superseded history.
Forbid independently editable ratification, locator, policy, inventory, migration, or process files from becoming parallel authorities after cutover.
Require generated artifacts to identify their input authorities and build-manifest hashes.
acceptance: Every artifact created by later phases has one classification.
No planned output duplicates an active fact owner.
tasks.fail_closed: A new artifact with ambiguous authority status cannot be committed.

pass_condition: Every governed fact group has exactly one proposed owner
All 10 registries have one proposed authority basis
exit_gate.on_fail: Stop data completion and ratification; resolve authority ambiguity first.

obligations.mapping_rationale: Mapped to universal obligation-coverage controls, source-class controls, phase-domain controls, and keyword-specific controls where applicable.

source_value_snapshot.name: Cluster, disposition, and burn down conflicts with evidence
source_value_snapshot.purpose: Apply the complete burn-down evidence protocol, cluster systematic defects, separate tooling and data work, resolve contract/module/orphan findings, and retain honest residual blockers.

tasks.title: Verify both predecessor plans are merged and the system is green
actions: Verify both predecessor plans are merged and the system is green.

tasks.title: Snapshot the exact work inventory. This plan was authored without knowing these numbers; every count below is discovered, not assumed
actions: Snapshot the exact work inventory. This plan was authored without knowing these numbers; every count below is discovered, not assumed.
Total baseline entries, broken down by registry and by verdict.
contract_registry: total records, needs_review count, conflict-queue entries referencing contracts.
module_registry: total records, count with empty responsibilities, count with empty process_step_ids, count at each confidence level, full list of stub module_ids.
registry_conflict_queue.jsonl: total open entries.
Orphan report: the two orphan classifications with their current state.
AMD-REG-001 outcome: whether dangling module.process_step_id references were baselined, and how many.
Current total record count across all registries (confirm or correct the 291 figure).
outputs: BURNDOWN_INVENTORY.md committed to the branch as the plan's own baseline of record

No estimate. Every figure is computed from committed files.

tasks.title: Implement a deterministic clustering report over the parity baseline
actions: Implement a deterministic clustering report over the parity baseline.
Group every non-agree parity record by (registry, authority_path, field, verdict).
For each cluster compute: cluster size, joined-record population for that mapping, and divergence_rate = size / population.
Emit clusters sorted by size descending, deterministically.
Pure analysis. Reads the parity report. Writes one generated artifact. Modifies no authored data.
The clustering report is a managed generated output: rendered, hashed, --check covered, byte-stable. It is evidence and must be reproducible.
No fuzzy grouping. Exact key tuples only.
acceptance: Every action and source-derived requirement in this task is completed.
All declared outputs and required evidence are produced or explicitly recorded as not applicable with approved rationale.
No fail-closed condition is triggered and the task leaves the repository within its approved write scope.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop this task and preserve evidence if any acceptance condition cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Apply systematic-defect heuristics to route clusters
actions: Apply systematic-defect heuristics to route clusters.
A heuristic ROUTES work. It never dispositions a record. Only PHASE-02 findings or PHASE-03 triage assign a disposition.
Heuristic thresholds are declared constants in the tooling, not judgement calls at review time.
A cluster routed to PHASE-02 that turns out NOT to be a tooling defect returns to PHASE-03 triage with that finding recorded.

actions.pattern: field_divergence rate >= 0.80 on a single field
actions.route: PHASE-02 adapter review, NOT per-record triage
actions.reason: Eighty percent of records being individually wrong in the same field is far less likely than one comparison rule being wrong.

actions.pattern: authority_only rate ~1.00 AND registry_only rate ~1.00 on the same mapping
actions.inference: join key format mismatch — the two sides never matched at all (e.g. M-01 vs M01, path separators, case)
actions.reason: This produces a maximally alarming report from a trivial defect, and no amount of data editing would ever fix it.

actions.inference: genuine registry under-coverage — the registry really is missing records

actions.pattern: field_divergence rate low and scattered across records
registry_only concentrated in one module or subsystem
actions.inference: possible authority staleness for that subsystem, or registry over-claim
actions.route: PHASE-03 triage with elevated attention; strong candidate for authority_stale_suspected

outputs: Routing table: cluster -> suspected class -> destination phase
acceptance: Every action and source-derived requirement in this task is completed.

tasks.fail_closed: Stop this task and preserve evidence if any acceptance condition cannot be proven.

tasks.title: For each PHASE-01 cluster routed as suspected systematic: prove or disprove the tooling defect by hand-checking at least 3 records in the cluster against primar
actions: For each PHASE-01 cluster routed as suspected systematic: prove or disprove the tooling defect by hand-checking at least 3 records in the cluster against primary sources.
Hand-check means: read the authority document, read the registry record, read the primary source, and state whether they actually disagree.
If they do not actually disagree, the comparison is defective. Proceed to fix.
If they do actually disagree, the cluster is genuine data divergence. Return it to PHASE-03 and record that the heuristic misrouted it.
acceptance: Every cluster has a written verdict: tooling_defect_confirmed or genuine_divergence_confirmed, with the hand-check evidence.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop this task and preserve evidence if any acceptance condition cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Correct confirmed adapter, normalization, and join-key defects in tools/registries/authority_projection.py
actions: Correct confirmed adapter, normalization, and join-key defects in tools/registries/authority_projection.py.
One PR per adapter defect.
Add a regression test reproducing the defect using a synthetic fixture before fixing it.

Normalization rule changes are declared in parity_authority_map.json ONLY IF that mapping change is approved as a governance change — otherwise the fix belongs in the adapter's projection, not the map.

Never widen normalization to make things match. Widening normalization (aggressive case folding, punctuation stripping, whitespace obliteration) is copying by another name: it hides real divergence inside the comparison rule. Every normalization change must be justified as reflecting a genuine formatting equivalence, not a semantic one.
acceptance: Every action and source-derived requirement in this task is completed.
All declared outputs and required evidence are produced or explicitly recorded as not applicable with approved rationale.
No fail-closed condition is triggered and the task leaves the repository within its approved write scope.

prohibited: Fuzzy matching, similarity thresholds, or any non-deterministic comparison (already prohibited by the parity plan; restated because this is the phase where the temptation peaks)
Normalizing away a difference that is actually meaningful

tasks.title: Re-derive the baseline for affected mappings only, under governed exception
actions: Re-derive the baseline for affected mappings only, under governed exception.
Re-derivation is scoped to the single mapping the adapter fix touched. No other mapping's baseline entries may change in that PR.
The PR must report, per verdict, the before and after counts for that mapping.
The PR must state the expected direction and magnitude of the change BEFORE showing the result, and reviewers confirm the actual delta matches the explanation.
A re-derivation that increases divergence count is not automatically wrong — a fixed adapter may reveal real divergence a broken one was hiding — but it must be explained.
An adapter fix whose baseline delta cannot be explained is not merged.
acceptance: Baseline delta fully explained by the adapter change.
No unrelated mapping's entries touched.
parity --check green afterwards.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop this task and preserve evidence if any acceptance condition cannot be proven.

source_detail.rationale: An adapter fix changes the comparison space, so parity_ids for that mapping may change wholesale. The parity plan forbids tooling from auto-appending to the baseline (PAR-051). This is the one sanctioned exception, and it is bounded.

tasks.title: Disposition every remaining baseline entry into exactly one class
actions: Disposition every remaining baseline entry into exactly one class.
Classification requires investigation: locate primary evidence (E1/E2) where it exists before choosing a class.
A disposition of registry_defect_accepted chosen without looking for primary evidence is a guess wearing a label. The disposition record must state what was searched.
Every disposition is authored into registry_authority_parity_baseline.json with a written rationale field.
No registry record is edited in this phase. Classification only.
acceptance: unreviewed disposition count = 0
Every entry carries a rationale
Every entry states which evidence classes were searched and what was found

source_detail.batching: Triage may be batched per registry. Triage PRs touch only the baseline file and are a distinct PR class from data PRs.

actions: Open tracking issues from triage output.
One governance issue per authority_stale_suspected finding, with evidence attached.
One code issue per code_defect_referred finding, naming both modules and the conflicting interface claims.

decision_gate.condition: If the E3-only forecast exceeds a declared threshold (recommend 50%) for a registry
decision_gate.action: Stop and escalate before burn-down. That registry's facts are not independently evidenced anywhere, which means burn-down would largely transcribe the authority. That is a finding about the system worth surfacing to the owner, not a task to grind through. It may indicate the registry should be a derived projection of the authority rather than an independent source.

tasks.title: For each conflicted or needs_review contract record, establish the actual interface from primary evidence
actions: For each conflicted or needs_review contract record, establish the actual interface from primary evidence.
Identify producer_module_id and consumer_module_id.
Locate the actual message/data structure in code or artifact: struct, schema, .mqh header, serializer, config contract.
Compare what the producer emits against what the consumer expects.
Record the finding before deciding the disposition.
Producer and consumer agree, registry record was wrong -> registry_defect_accepted. Correct the record with E1/E2 source_refs, clear needs_review, remove the baseline entry.

Producer and consumer genuinely disagree -> code_defect_referred. Record the true conflicting state in the registry with evidence from BOTH sides. Open a code issue. Keep the baseline entry with this disposition. Do NOT invent a reconciled interface that exists in neither module.
Neither side has locatable primary evidence -> resolve on E3 with confidence medium, tag derived_from_authority_only, and record that primary evidence was searched for and not found.

source_detail.note: Contract registry cutover per EAFIX-REGISTRY-AUTHORITY-PARITY-001 cutover_criteria requires an EMPTY baseline. code_defect_referred entries therefore block contract cutover until the underlying code issues close. That is correct: a registry accurately recording a broken interface should not be promoted to authoritative-and-blocking while the interface is still broken. Surface this to the owner as a cutover dependency rather than resolving it here.
source_detail.escalation: If code_defect_referred entries would indefinitely block contract cutover, that is a governance decision for the owner — either fix the code, or define a cutover exception class with recorded rationale. This plan does not make that call.

tasks.title: For each stub module identified in BURN-002, locate primary evidence for responsibilities and process_step_ids
actions: For each stub module identified in BURN-002, locate primary evidence for responsibilities and process_step_ids.
Use file_module_mapping.csv and the services reference to locate the module's implementation files.
Derive responsibilities from what the code actually does, corroborated by the module catalog.
Derive process_step_ids from actual call sites, handlers, and message flow, validated against process_registry IDs.
Implementation exists -> populate from E1 evidence, confidence high.

Implementation does not exist (planned module) -> set an explicit planned/not_implemented status rather than leaving fields empty. An empty field says 'we do not know'; a planned status says 'there is nothing to know yet'. These are different facts and the registry must distinguish them.
Implementation exists but responsibilities are only describable from the catalog -> populate at confidence medium, tagged derived_from_authority_only.

source_value_snapshot.title: Apply systematic-defect heuristics to route clusters
actions: Apply systematic-defect heuristics to route clusters.
A heuristic ROUTES work. It never dispositions a record. Only PHASE-02 findings or PHASE-03 triage assign a disposition.
Heuristic thresholds are declared constants in the tooling, not judgement calls at review time.
A cluster routed to PHASE-02 that turns out NOT to be a tooling defect returns to PHASE-03 triage with that finding recorded.

actions.pattern: field_divergence rate >= 0.80 on a single field
actions.route: PHASE-02 adapter review, NOT per-record triage
actions.reason: Eighty percent of records being individually wrong in the same field is far less likely than one comparison rule being wrong.

actions.pattern: authority_only rate ~1.00 AND registry_only rate ~1.00 on the same mapping
actions.inference: join key format mismatch — the two sides never matched at all (e.g. M-01 vs M01, path separators, case)
actions.reason: This produces a maximally alarming report from a trivial defect, and no amount of data editing would ever fix it.

actions.inference: genuine registry under-coverage — the registry really is missing records

actions.pattern: field_divergence rate low and scattered across records
registry_only concentrated in one module or subsystem
actions.inference: possible authority staleness for that subsystem, or registry over-claim
actions.route: PHASE-03 triage with elevated attention; strong candidate for authority_stale_suspected

source_value_snapshot.title: For each PHASE-01 cluster routed as suspected systematic: prove or disprove the tooling defect by hand-checking at least 3 records in the cluster against primar
actions: For each PHASE-01 cluster routed as suspected systematic: prove or disprove the tooling defect by hand-checking at least 3 records in the cluster against primary sources.
Hand-check means: read the authority document, read the registry record, read the primary source, and state whether they actually disagree.
If they do not actually disagree, the comparison is defective. Proceed to fix.
If they do actually disagree, the cluster is genuine data divergence. Return it to PHASE-03 and record that the heuristic misrouted it.

source_value_snapshot.title: Correct confirmed adapter, normalization, and join-key defects in tools/registries/authority_projection.py
actions: Correct confirmed adapter, normalization, and join-key defects in tools/registries/authority_projection.py.
One PR per adapter defect.
Add a regression test reproducing the defect using a synthetic fixture before fixing it.

Normalization rule changes are declared in parity_authority_map.json ONLY IF that mapping change is approved as a governance change — otherwise the fix belongs in the adapter's projection, not the map.

Never widen normalization to make things match. Widening normalization (aggressive case folding, punctuation stripping, whitespace obliteration) is copying by another name: it hides real divergence inside the comparison rule. Every normalization change must be justified as reflecting a genuine formatting equivalence, not a semantic one.

obligations.requirement: Verify task SSOT-0408 (Re-derive the baseline for affected mappings only, under governed exception) satisfied preconditions, dependencies, actions, write scope, outputs, acceptance, evidence, human-gate, and completion-record requirements.

source_value_snapshot.title: Re-derive the baseline for affected mappings only, under governed exception
actions: Re-derive the baseline for affected mappings only, under governed exception.
Re-derivation is scoped to the single mapping the adapter fix touched. No other mapping's baseline entries may change in that PR.
The PR must report, per verdict, the before and after counts for that mapping.
The PR must state the expected direction and magnitude of the change BEFORE showing the result, and reviewers confirm the actual delta matches the explanation.
A re-derivation that increases divergence count is not automatically wrong — a fixed adapter may reveal real divergence a broken one was hiding — but it must be explained.
An adapter fix whose baseline delta cannot be explained is not merged.
acceptance: Baseline delta fully explained by the adapter change.
No unrelated mapping's entries touched.
parity --check green afterwards.
source_value_snapshot.fail_closed: Stop this task and preserve evidence if any acceptance condition cannot be proven.

source_detail.rationale: An adapter fix changes the comparison space, so parity_ids for that mapping may change wholesale. The parity plan forbids tooling from auto-appending to the baseline (PAR-051). This is the one sanctioned exception, and it is bounded.

obligations.requirement: Verify task SSOT-0412 (For each conflicted or needs_review contract record, establish the actual interface from primary evidence) satisfied preconditions, dependencies, actions, write scope, outputs, acceptance, evidence, human-gate, and completion-record requirements.

source_value_snapshot.title: For each conflicted or needs_review contract record, establish the actual interface from primary evidence
actions: For each conflicted or needs_review contract record, establish the actual interface from primary evidence.
Identify producer_module_id and consumer_module_id.
Locate the actual message/data structure in code or artifact: struct, schema, .mqh header, serializer, config contract.
Compare what the producer emits against what the consumer expects.
Record the finding before deciding the disposition.
Producer and consumer agree, registry record was wrong -> registry_defect_accepted. Correct the record with E1/E2 source_refs, clear needs_review, remove the baseline entry.

Producer and consumer genuinely disagree -> code_defect_referred. Record the true conflicting state in the registry with evidence from BOTH sides. Open a code issue. Keep the baseline entry with this disposition. Do NOT invent a reconciled interface that exists in neither module.
Neither side has locatable primary evidence -> resolve on E3 with confidence medium, tag derived_from_authority_only, and record that primary evidence was searched for and not found.

source_detail.note: Contract registry cutover per EAFIX-REGISTRY-AUTHORITY-PARITY-001 cutover_criteria requires an EMPTY baseline. code_defect_referred entries therefore block contract cutover until the underlying code issues close. That is correct: a registry accurately recording a broken interface should not be promoted to authoritative-and-blocking while the interface is still broken. Surface this to the owner as a cutover dependency rather than resolving it here.
source_detail.escalation: If code_defect_referred entries would indefinitely block contract cutover, that is a governance decision for the owner — either fix the code, or define a cutover exception class with recorded rationale. This plan does not make that call.

obligations.requirement: Verify task SSOT-0414 (For each stub module identified in BURN-002, locate primary evidence for responsibilities and process_step_ids) satisfied preconditions, dependencies, actions, write scope, outputs, acceptance, evidence, human-gate, and completion-record requirements.

source_value_snapshot.title: For each stub module identified in BURN-002, locate primary evidence for responsibilities and process_step_ids
actions: For each stub module identified in BURN-002, locate primary evidence for responsibilities and process_step_ids.
Use file_module_mapping.csv and the services reference to locate the module's implementation files.
Derive responsibilities from what the code actually does, corroborated by the module catalog.
Derive process_step_ids from actual call sites, handlers, and message flow, validated against process_registry IDs.
Implementation exists -> populate from E1 evidence, confidence high.

Implementation does not exist (planned module) -> set an explicit planned/not_implemented status rather than leaving fields empty. An empty field says 'we do not know'; a planned status says 'there is nothing to know yet'. These are different facts and the registry must distinguish them.
Implementation exists but responsibilities are only describable from the catalog -> populate at confidence medium, tagged derived_from_authority_only.

needs_review_fields empty across module_registry

actions: Honesty audit. This is the task that determines whether the burn-down was real.
outputs: BURNDOWN_EXIT_REPORT.md with all counts, the evidence-class ratio, and the delta against BURNDOWN_INVENTORY.md
acceptance: Every action and source-derived requirement in this task is completed.
All declared outputs and required evidence are produced or explicitly recorded as not applicable with approved rationale.
No fail-closed condition is triggered and the task leaves the repository within its approved write scope.
source_value_snapshot.fail_closed: Stop this task and preserve evidence if any acceptance condition cannot be proven.

checks: Ratio of resolutions carrying E1/E2 primary evidence versus E3-only, per registry. Publish it. A high E3-only ratio means the registry largely transcribes the authority and its cutover value is correspondingly reduced.
Count of authority_stale_suspected findings. Zero across a large burn-down is a red flag, not a success: it means either the authority is perfect or nobody looked at primary evidence.
Count of code_defect_referred findings. Publish with issue links.
Count of records tagged derived_from_authority_only. Publish per registry.
Confirm zero authority documents, routing files, doc_authority.json, registry_index.json, parity_authority_map.json, or schema files were modified.
Confirm every baseline removal has a corresponding resolves_parity_id in a committed record's source_refs.
source_detail.rule: The exit report states what was actually learned about the system, not merely that the numbers reached zero. A burn-down that produced no findings about the codebase did not investigate it.

obligations.requirement: Verify phase PHASE-05 (Complete authority coverage and close schema/comparison gaps) executed after its dependencies, all tasks were covered, and its exit gate controlled progression.

source_value_snapshot.name: Complete authority coverage and close schema/comparison gaps
source_value_snapshot.purpose: Establish a deterministic authority or substitute evidence basis for the six formerly uncovered registries and close every required unrepresentable or not-comparable gap.

tasks.title: Establish an authority basis for the six no_declared_authority registries
actions: integration, configuration, operational_control, operator, reuse, and decision registries cannot cut over on parity evidence because nothing external verifies them. For each, choose exactly one path: (a) nominate an existing document as its parity authority via doc_authority.json and add the mapping + adapter, (b) declare the registry born-canonical because no prior document exists — verified instead by targeted evidence checks (e.g. configuration records verified against actual config files in the repo; operational controls against deployed risk-control code), or (c) retire the registry as not needed, recorded in decision_registry.
outputs: New adapters and evidence validators as required
decision_registry records for every born-canonical or retirement decision
acceptance: 0 registries with unresolved authority basis
tasks.fail_closed: Stop this task and preserve evidence if any acceptance condition cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Establish the integration-registry authority basis
actions: Evaluate communication_channels.json and routed transport authorities as parity authorities for integration identities, protocols, directions, ports, producers, consumers, and contract links.
Where no predecessor authority exists, define substitute evidence validators against implementation, schemas, and configuration.
Record the selected basis in decision_registry.
acceptance: Exactly one authority basis is selected.
Every claimed integration fact has a deterministic evidence path.
tasks.fail_closed: Integration registry cannot cut over without an approved basis.

tasks.title: Establish the configuration-registry authority basis
actions: Validate configuration records against committed config files, schemas, defaults, environment contracts, and runtime loaders.
Choose predecessor authority, born-canonical with evidence validators, or retirement.
Record secrets and runtime-only values as references or classifications, never copied secrets.
acceptance: Every configuration record class has an evidence source.
No secret value is moved into registry data.
tasks.fail_closed: Configuration registry cannot cut over without deterministic evidence.

tasks.title: Establish the operational-control-registry authority basis
actions: Validate control identities and relationships against active risk, safety, lifecycle, deployment, and fail-closed implementation evidence.
Keep detailed control logic in its routed subject authority or implementation when it is not suitable for registry duplication.
Record the selected authority basis.
acceptance: Every active control has evidence and an owner.
Fail-closed controls are not weakened by registry abstraction.
tasks.fail_closed: Unverified safety-control records block operational-control cutover.

tasks.title: Establish the operator-registry authority basis
actions: Use the routed UI catalog for operator-facing entity identities and relationships where declared authoritative.
Keep detailed UI behavior, disabled-state rules, and safety levels in the registered UI authority unless explicitly migrated.
Define substitute evidence checks against actual REST/WebSocket/UI implementation for fields without a predecessor authority.
acceptance: UI authority and operator registry fact groups do not overlap.
Every operator record has a deterministic evidence path.
tasks.fail_closed: Operator registry cannot cut over while UI ownership overlaps.

tasks.title: Establish the reuse-registry authority basis
actions: Validate reuse records against the capability registry, pattern registry, reusable-program registry, dependency manifests, and implementation evidence.
Apply the project's reuse-before-custom-development policy.
Choose predecessor authority, born-canonical with evidence validators, or retirement.
acceptance: Every reusable capability has evidence and lifecycle status.
No registry entry claims a nonexistent reusable component.
tasks.fail_closed: Reuse registry cannot cut over without an evidence-backed basis.

tasks.title: Establish the decision-registry authority basis
actions: Declare the decision registry born-canonical only for approved decisions created under this governance model.
Import historical decisions only with immutable source evidence and explicit lifecycle classification.
Keep unresolved proposals and implementation-plan statements non-authoritative.
acceptance: Approved decisions are distinguishable from proposals and inferred conclusions.
Every decision record has approver, date, rationale, scope, and supersession behavior.
tasks.fail_closed: The decision registry cannot be authoritative for undocumented or unapproved decisions.

tasks.title: Close schema gaps (unrepresentable) and comparison gaps (not_comparable)
actions: For every unrepresentable verdict: extend the registry schema so the fact can be expressed, then populate it. For every not_comparable field: either define a deterministic normalization that makes it comparable, or record an accepted disposition with written rationale. Schema changes go through the normal schema + validator + regeneration cycle.
acceptance: unrepresentable = 0 for all cutover-track registries; every not_comparable field dispositioned
tasks.fail_closed: Stop this task and preserve evidence if any acceptance condition cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Close all authority-basis, unrepresentable, and not-comparable gaps
actions: Update authority mappings and adapters only through governed changes.
Extend schemas only when a fact must be represented in the registry and no retained external authority owns it.
Disposition every not_comparable field with deterministic normalization or written acceptance.
Regenerate parity and coverage reports.
acceptance: Registries with unresolved authority basis = 0.
Unrepresentable facts for cutover-track registries = 0.
Every not_comparable fact is dispositioned and visible.
No retained external authority's detailed facts are duplicated into a registry.
tasks.fail_closed: Any unresolved authority basis or unrepresentable required fact blocks affected registry completion.

pass_condition: Unrepresentable required facts = 0 for target registries
Retained external authorities do not overlap registry ownership
exit_gate.on_fail: Block only affected registry completion and cutover; do not fabricate a basis.

source_value_snapshot.purpose: Resolve duplicate naming plans, reconcile 27/28/34 identities, ratify 34 IDs/symbols/locators, and close naming, packaging, schema, state, and ownership decision prerequisites.

tasks.title: Select the canonical naming-plan document
actions: Select the canonical naming-plan document
Choose the surviving filename.
Mark the duplicate as superseded or delete it through documentation governance.
Update doc authority and routing only after the human decision.
acceptance: Exactly one active naming-plan authority remains.
No routing or authority record points to the retired duplicate.

Produce one row per identity with current ID, symbols, aliases, status, source authority, and unresolved conflict flags.

Every old identity is mapped, retired, aliased, or explicitly unresolved.

Preserve numeric module ID 50000000000000000025.

tasks.fail_closed: Stop this task and preserve evidence if any acceptance condition cannot be proven.

Update routing and document authority to recognize the ratified source.
acceptance: Active module count equals 34.
IDs, symbols, and locators are each unique.

tasks.title: Commit the ratified 34-module identity through the module registry authority model

source_refs.filename: EAFIX Module Folder Naming and Migration Plan.txt

preconditions: HD module identity, F4, locator, and naming decisions are approved.
actions: Make module_registry.jsonl the candidate owner of module identity, canonical symbol, permanent locator, lifecycle, aliases, and relationships.
Represent module_identity_ratification and module_locator_assignments as immutable approval evidence or generated projections, not independently editable authorities.
Generate compatibility projections required by existing tools, including a populated current 34-module catalog.
Update routing and doc authority only during the later cutover phase.
outputs: approved module registry records for 34 modules
acceptance: Active/canonical candidate module count = 34.
Module IDs, canonical symbols, and locators are each unique.
Locators are explicit, permanent, never runtime-derived, and never reused.
No standalone ratification file competes with module_registry.
tasks.fail_closed: Do not populate or route a 34-module authority before ratification.

tasks.title: Validate the ratified module universe against manifests, process bindings, and routing
actions: Validate all 34 module manifests and module registry records against the approved crosswalk.
Validate module process_step_ids, dependencies, aliases, naming rules, and locator uniqueness.
Classify planned or unimplemented modules explicitly instead of fabricating responsibilities or high confidence.
Regenerate candidate projections and review all diffs.
acceptance: 34 module identities accounted for.
No duplicate ID, symbol, locator, or active alias collision.
No invalid process reference.
No low-confidence or empty implemented-module records remain; planned modules are explicitly marked planned.
tasks.fail_closed: Any unresolved identity collision or fabricated module fact blocks phase completion.

pass_condition: Naming separator and packaging decisions approved
No standalone ratification file competes with module registry
exit_gate.on_fail: Stop module completion and repository migration design.

source_value_snapshot.purpose: Populate every bounded registry with evidence-backed records, cross-registry links, governed counts, and explicit readiness states.

tasks.title: Complete module registry
actions: Populate all 34 module identities, locators, aliases, statuses, responsibilities, dependencies, process links, service/runtime links, profile refs, and authority refs.
Represent non-process modules without fabricated process steps.
acceptance: 34 active module records.
All module references valid.
No invented step links.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop the task and preserve evidence if acceptance cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Complete process registry
actions: Populate canonical steps, codes, order, phase, dependencies, module owners, input/output contract IDs, validation controls, and failure controls.
Reconcile process-step and aligned-process authorities.
acceptance: Step IDs and codes unique.
Phase and dependency graph acyclic where required.
Module and contract references valid.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop the task and preserve evidence if acceptance cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Complete contract registry without duplicating shapes
actions: Register input and output contracts, versions, producers, consumers, process links, schema refs, implementation status, examples, and compatibility policy.
Keep field definitions in canonical schemas.
Record unresolved contracts as blocking for affected buildability.
acceptance: Every contract link resolves.
One canonical schema per shape.
No field-shape duplication.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop the task and preserve evidence if acceptance cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Complete artifact registry baseline
actions: Inventory every tracked path plus approved runtime/generated exclusions.
Store repository-relative POSIX paths, path type, owner, consumers, source/generated classification, hashes, and authority refs.
Replace absolute Windows paths in mapping inputs.
acceptance: Every tracked path represented once.
No absolute path remains.
Multi-owner/unmapped records are explicit.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop the task and preserve evidence if acceptance cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Complete integration and operator registries
actions: Register channels, protocols, ports, directions, producers, consumers, contracts, UI entities, APIs, WebSockets, safety controls, and specialized authority refs.
Preserve MT4 and UI authority boundaries.
acceptance: Channel and UI IDs unique.
Ports/protocols match routed authorities or have decisions.
All links resolve.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop the task and preserve evidence if acceptance cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Complete configuration, operational-control, reuse, and decision registries
actions: Register configuration identities and owners, safety/failure controls, existing reusable capabilities, and all approval decisions from prior phases.
Search capability registry before custom implementation records are accepted.
acceptance: All prior human decisions have stable IDs.
Every custom-solution record explains why reuse was insufficient.
Safety controls have owners and validation refs.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop the task and preserve evidence if acceptance cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Run full cross-registry completeness and orphan analysis
actions: Generate graph and reports.
Resolve duplicate IDs, broken references, orphaned required records, missing provenance, and incompatible status transitions.
Preserve approved external-authority links.
acceptance: Schema errors = 0.
Duplicate IDs = 0.
Enabled foreign-reference errors = 0.
Unapproved orphans = 0.
Required provenance gaps = 0.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop the task and preserve evidence if acceptance cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Apply evidence-class confidence ceilings across all registry records
actions: Require E1 code or E2 concrete artifact evidence for confidence high factual claims.
Limit E3 authority-only facts to confidence medium and tag derived_from_authority_only.
Use E4 historical evidence only as supporting evidence.
Use E5 human judgement only for approved future-state decisions, never as proof of current implementation.
Require path, commit SHA, locator, observation time, and linked parity/decision ID where applicable.
acceptance: Every active factual record has resolvable evidence.
No E3-only record is labeled confidence high.
No historical or judgement source is represented as current implementation evidence.
tasks.fail_closed: Unsupported high-confidence records block affected registry completion.

tasks.title: Govern expected counts without hardcoded permanent totals
actions: Maintain registry_expected_counts.json as a reviewed exact-count contract.
Require every intentional count change to identify prior count, new count, delta, and the evidence/decision IDs justifying additions or retirement transitions.
Never delete records to reduce a count; use lifecycle transitions.
Block unexplained count changes.
acceptance: Committed expected counts equal current authored records.
Every count delta is evidence-linked.
No source-plan literal such as 291 is treated as permanent.
tasks.fail_closed: Any unexplained count delta blocks merge.

tasks.title: Prove all ten domain registries are complete candidates
