obligations.requirement: Verify phase PHASE-08 (Consolidate operational process information into registry-backed projections) executed after its dependencies, all tasks were covered, and its exit gate controlled progression.

source_value_snapshot.name: Consolidate operational process information into registry-backed projections
source_value_snapshot.purpose: Mine the full process-document universe, reconcile the complete process field superset, ratify process_registry as the single authored process authority, and generate operational views.

tasks.title: Inventory the complete process-document universe deterministically
preconditions: Routing references are resolved and archive exclusions are known.
actions: Enumerate all files in approved process-document paths.
Search for process, phase, step, flow, lifecycle, runbook, procedure, work cell, state, branch, validation, failure, input, output, trigger, and handoff content.
Exclude archived and superseded paths by default.
Classify each candidate by domain, scope, authority, lifecycle, and evidence type.
Confirm no current process source is omitted.
acceptance: Every current process-related file is inventoried or explicitly excluded with reason.
Each inventory record includes path, hash, authority, scope, and lifecycle status.
tasks.fail_closed: Any unexplained omitted current process source blocks consolidation.

tasks.title: Define the canonical process field superset and schema mapping
actions: Model every field group in sections 4.1 through 4.26 of the process-consolidation specification.

Map process identity, purpose, boundaries, phases, steps, triggers, guards, contracts, activities, branches, states, validation, failure, retry, determinism, transport, persistence, configuration, timing, risk, observability, implementation evidence, runbooks, UI bindings, traceability, conflicts, and gaps.
Assign each field group to process_registry, another registry, or a registered external authority.
Do not duplicate contract field shapes, UI behavior, MT4 facts, calendar deterministic rules, or other retained subject authorities.
acceptance: Every process superset field has exactly one fact owner.
The process registry can reference all required detail without duplicating retained external authorities.
tasks.fail_closed: Ambiguous ownership of any process field blocks extraction.

tasks.title: Extract and normalize process fields with source-level provenance
actions: Extract headings and structured keys from every inventoried source.
Map each source fact to the canonical superset field.
Record unique values, duplicates, conflicts, source locations, and hashes.
Mark unstructured or ambiguous facts for manual review.
Keep required behavior, current behavior, planned behavior, examples, and historical behavior distinct.
acceptance: Every extracted fact has source path, locator, hash, authority rank, and behavior status.
Examples and aspirations are not promoted to requirements.
tasks.fail_closed: Facts without source provenance cannot enter the process registry.

tasks.title: Reconcile canonical steps, modules, contracts, channels, states, and runtime evidence
preconditions: The ratified 34-module candidate universe and complete registries are available.
actions: Start from the routed aligned process and process-step catalog.

Attach ratified module identities, subsystem details, module-manifest details, work-cell activities, source/test evidence, implementation status, contracts, channels, stores, timing, validation, failure behavior, runbooks, conflicts, and gaps.
Use stable registry IDs for all cross-domain references.
Represent planned or missing implementation explicitly.
acceptance: Every canonical process step has one identity and complete cross-registry links.
No step depends on an unratified module or unresolved ID alias.
tasks.fail_closed: Unresolved step identity or module binding blocks process ratification.

tasks.title: Reconcile cross-cutting operational policies once
actions: Reconcile time standard, identifier systems, checksum policy, idempotency, file sequence, atomic writes, transport hierarchy, retry policy, stale-data behavior, health, heartbeat, logging, traceability, risk-off default, operator override, security, and permissions.
Store policy identities and relationships in the proper registries and reference detailed subject authorities.
Record explicit conflicts instead of silently selecting lower-authority values.
acceptance: Each cross-cutting policy has one owner and one active rule source.
No duplicate policy prose is copied into every step.
tasks.fail_closed: Unresolved safety, identity, timing, or transport conflicts block process completion.

tasks.title: Validate the consolidated process graph and evidence model
actions: Validate unique process, phase, and step IDs; module and dependency references; orphan inputs; undeclared outputs; channel, contract, and state references; failure behavior; blocking-gate evidence; implementation evidence; planned markers; conflicts; runbooks; source references; and deterministic regeneration.
Validate acyclic or explicitly governed dependency and state graphs.
Validate that every process projection is derived from process_registry plus registered external authorities.
acceptance: Unique and reference validation errors = 0.
Every failure has behavior.
Every blocking gate has evidence.
Every planned claim is marked planned.
Every conflict and implementation gap is visible.
Two regenerations are byte-identical.
tasks.fail_closed: Any hidden gap, orphan, unresolved source, or nondeterministic projection blocks ratification.

tasks.title: Ratify process_registry as the sole authored operational-process authority
preconditions: Process validation passes.
actions: Approve process_registry.jsonl as the authored owner of process identities, phases, steps, order, dependencies, module bindings, and cross-registry references.
Classify eafix_operational_process.current.json as a generated enriched projection, not a second authored authority.
Record the process authority model in decision_registry and registry_authority_manifest.
acceptance: Exactly one authored process authority exists.
The operational-process JSON and Markdown are generated and protected from manual edits.
tasks.fail_closed: Do not proceed to cutover while process_registry and operational-process files compete for authority.

tasks.title: Generate operational-process projections and matrices
actions: Generate eafix_operational_process.current.json, EAFIX_OPERATIONAL_PROCESS.generated.md, process_flow.mmd, process_flow.svg, process_contract_matrix.csv, process_failure_matrix.csv, process_implementation_gap_report.json, and module/phase/operator process views.
Register every output in the managed-output manifest and no-write check mode.
Ensure projections introduce no facts absent from their approved inputs.
acceptance: All projections are deterministic and manifest-hashed.
Manual edits are detected.
Projection facts trace to registry or registered external authority.
tasks.fail_closed: Unmanaged or fact-creating projections block phase completion.

tasks.title: Prepare the process-source supersession inventory without executing cutover
actions: Classify each legacy process narrative as superseded candidate, retained subsystem runbook, retained specialized authority, archived history, or unresolved.
Generate proposed doc_authority and routing changes for later atomic cutover.
Preserve Git history and subsystem procedures.
acceptance: Every process source has one proposed disposition.
No authority file is changed in this phase.
tasks.fail_closed: Unclassified process sources block process cutover.

pass_condition: All process IDs and references valid
Every failure and gate has behavior/evidence
Generated operational process and matrices are deterministic
Every legacy process source has a proposed disposition
exit_gate.on_fail: Stop stability and cutover until process authority ambiguity and validation failures are resolved.

obligations.requirement: Verify phase PHASE-09 (Prove sustained green state and prepare cutover) executed after its dependencies, all tasks were covered, and its exit gate controlled progression.

source_value_snapshot.name: Prove sustained green state and prepare cutover
source_value_snapshot.purpose: Hold qualified registries green across normal commits, resolve every residual blocker, and test the complete forward/rollback package.

tasks.title: Prove sustained green parity per registry
actions: For each registry meeting the cutover criteria (baseline empty, authority_only = 0, field_divergence = 0, unrepresentable = 0, registry_only dispositioned), hold it green across the declared minimum consecutive commits (recommend 10) with normal repository activity continuing. This proves stability under change, not a lucky snapshot.
acceptance: Per-registry green-streak counter reaches threshold, tracked in a generated stability report
tasks.fail_closed: Stop this task and preserve evidence if any acceptance condition cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Track a per-registry sustained-green observation window
preconditions: Registry candidate readiness and process consolidation are complete.
actions: Track consecutive qualifying commits per registry.
Count a commit only when structural validation, parity, evidence, authority basis, and generated-output checks execute and pass.
Reset only the affected registry's streak on a relevant regression.
Use a governed threshold, recommended ten qualifying commits, approved before observation.
acceptance: Every target registry reaches the approved threshold.
No skipped or startup-blocked run counts toward a streak.
tasks.fail_closed: A registry without a proven green streak cannot cut over.

tasks.title: Resolve every residual parity, authority, or code blocker before cutover
actions: Close authority_stale_suspected items through governed authority updates or explicit cutover decisions.
Close code_defect_referred items through bounded implementation fixes and evidence refresh.
Tighten the parity baseline record-by-record as findings resolve.
Do not auto-regenerate or bulk-clear baseline entries.
acceptance: Baseline entries for each target registry = 0.
Authority-only, field-divergence, and unrepresentable counts = 0.
Every registry-only and not-comparable item has an approved disposition.
tasks.fail_closed: Any unresolved baseline entry blocks that registry's cutover.

tasks.title: Generate and rehearse the complete cutover and rollback package
actions: Generate exact per-registry changes to registry_index, registry_authority_manifest, doc_authority, routing, compatibility projections, supersession banners, CI checks, and downstream pointers.
Generate the inverse rollback patch.
Test rollback in a clean disposable clone without deleting predecessor documents.
Record dependency order: module, process, contract, artifact, then remaining registries as qualified.
acceptance: Forward and rollback patches apply cleanly in a clean clone.
Rollback restores previous authority pointers and passing validation.
tasks.fail_closed: Untested rollback blocks cutover approval.

pass_condition: Approved green-streak threshold met per target registry
No authority_only, field_divergence, or unrepresentable facts
Forward and rollback packages pass in a clean clone
exit_gate.on_fail: Do not approve cutover for any failed registry.

obligations.mapping_rationale: Mapped to universal obligation-coverage controls, source-class controls, phase-domain controls, and keyword-specific controls where applicable.

obligations.requirement: Verify phase PHASE-10 (Execute dependency-ordered per-registry authority cutover) executed after its dependencies, all tasks were covered, and its exit gate controlled progression.

source_value_snapshot.name: Execute dependency-ordered per-registry authority cutover
source_value_snapshot.purpose: Apply one governed, reversible cutover at a time, update authority pointers atomically, retain predecessors, and remove shadow escape hatches only after qualification.

tasks.title: Generate cutover diff and rollback package
actions: Generate exact routing, doc-authority, authority-manifest, current-pointer, and compatibility-projection changes.
Create inverse patch restoring previous authority pointers.
Prove rollback in a temporary branch or worktree.
acceptance: Cutover and rollback patches are deterministic.
Rollback restores the prior authority model without data loss.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop the task and preserve evidence if acceptance cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Review field-level authority coverage
actions: Confirm every governed fact group has one canonical owner after cutover.
Confirm retained external authorities are registered and non-overlapping.
Confirm old plans and catalogs are classified as generated, supporting, superseded, or compatibility projections.
acceptance: No dual active authority.
No fact group lacks an owner.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop the task and preserve evidence if acceptance cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Approve authority cutover
actions: Record HD-SSOT-011.
Review candidate integrity, parity baseline, registry completeness, and rollback evidence.
Approve exact commit scope.
acceptance: Human approval references frozen hashes and patch.
No physical module move is included.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop the task and preserve evidence if acceptance cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Update routing and document authority atomically
actions: Activate registry authority manifest.
Point module/process/artifact/contract/etc. routes to canonical registry sources or registered external authorities.
Reclassify former independent catalogs according to approved compatibility policy.
Register generated current views.
acceptance: All authority pointers resolve.
No stale active path remains.
Candidate registries are no longer described as shadow.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop the task and preserve evidence if acceptance cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Validate cutover from a clean clone
actions: Clone the cutover commit.
Run routing resolution, candidate integrity, parity, authority coverage, and generated-output checks.
Run rollback rehearsal once more.
acceptance: All checks pass.
Rollback remains executable.
No working-tree drift.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop the task and preserve evidence if acceptance cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Remove report-only mode and retire shadow scaffolding
actions: Once all cutover-track registries are canonical: delete continue-on-error and --report-only paths from CI for those registries, rename the workflow to drop '(Shadow)', and make the full validation suite (structural + parity + authority-state) a required check for merge. The parity job remains permanently — it now guards against the canonical registries drifting from any remaining authoritative documents and from each other.
acceptance: CI has no advisory escape hatch for canonical registries; validation is a required branch-protection check
tasks.fail_closed: Stop this task and preserve evidence if any acceptance condition cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Execute dependency-ordered per-registry authority cutovers
preconditions: The approved cutover package and rollback rehearsal pass.
actions: Cut over one qualified registry or tightly coupled dependency group per bounded PR.
Set authority_status canonical and approved_for_cutover true only for the target registry.
Activate the authority manifest mapping for that registry.
Supersede predecessor documents with pointers but retain them for audit and rollback.
Regenerate and review every compatibility projection.
acceptance: Only qualified registries change status.
All current pointers resolve.
No physical repository migration occurs in a cutover PR.
tasks.fail_closed: A failed registry cutover is rolled back independently; later registry cutovers stop.

tasks.title: Validate the complete authority state from a clean clone
actions: Clone at the cutover commit.
Resolve every routing and doc-authority pointer.
Run structural, parity, authority-state, process-projection, and generated-output validation.
Verify predecessor documents are retained and marked superseded.
Verify canonical registry validation is blocking.
acceptance: All cutover registries are canonical and green.
All non-cutover registries retain explicit status.
No stale authority pointer remains.
tasks.fail_closed: Apply the tested rollback package if authority resolution or validation fails.

pass_condition: Each cutover registry is canonical and approved
Authority manifest, routing, doc authority, and compatibility pointers agree
exit_gate.on_fail: Apply the tested rollback for the failed registry and stop later cutovers.

tasks.title: Resolve manifest filename convention
actions: Resolve manifest filename convention
Apply HD-03.
Update the naming specification and all target-tree templates to the accepted separator.
Add a validator for manifest filename, module ID, canonical symbol, and .module-id agreement.
acceptance: One manifest filename convention is active.
All generated module paths use the same convention.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop this task and preserve evidence if any acceptance condition cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Choose packaging model
actions: Choose packaging model
Inventory current package/import/build behavior.
Evaluate root-only, per-module, and workspace packaging against runtime and deployment requirements.
Record the accepted model and migration consequences.
acceptance: The target tree contains pyproject.toml files only where the decision requires them.
Build, install, test, and import commands are defined for the selected model.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop this task and preserve evidence if any acceptance condition cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Define module-root artifact inventory
actions: Define module-root artifact inventory
Classify .module-id, manifest, README.md, AGENTS.md, pyproject.toml, and other root files as required, optional, generated, or prohibited by profile.
Define provenance and generation rules for README.md and AGENTS.md.
Forbid creation of empty generic context files without evidence.
acceptance: Every module has an explicit root-artifact set.
Every required artifact has an owner, source, and validation rule.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop this task and preserve evidence if any acceptance condition cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Create module container inventory
actions: Create module container inventory
Derive current implementation needs from manifests, file mapping, runtime evidence, tests, and documentation.
Assign only first-level containers actually required by each module.
Record not_applicable reasons for omitted baseline containers.
acceptance: All 34 modules appear exactly once.
No unsupported container variant is used.
No empty folder is required solely for visual uniformity.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop this task and preserve evidence if any acceptance condition cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Create nested module profiles
actions: Create nested module profiles
Ratify Python, MT4-native, mixed-runtime, UI full-stack, shared-kernel, and documentation-only profiles.
Assign exactly one primary profile to each module.
Record evidence-backed extensions for exceptional modules.
acceptance: Every proposed nested folder is authorized by a profile or explicit extension.
Runtime-constrained directory names remain unchanged.

tasks.title: Inventory and classify non-module top-level paths
actions: Inventory and classify non-module top-level paths
Inventory architecture, contracts, governance, integration-tests, tools, EAFIX_auth_docs, .github, .state, and all other top-level paths.
Classify each as canonical global root, migration candidate, compatibility root, generated root, runtime-only root, or archive.
acceptance: Every current and proposed non-module root is classified.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop this task and preserve evidence if any acceptance condition cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Approve global root policy
actions: Approve global root policy
Define purpose, owner, allowed content, prohibited content, naming, validation, and migration rules for each global root.
Define the relationship between integration-tests and module-local tests.
acceptance: No global root exists solely because it appeared in the proposed tree.
Every retained global root has an explicit authority and owner.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop this task and preserve evidence if any acceptance condition cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Approve schema placement policy
actions: Approve schema placement policy
Classify schemas as cross-module canonical, external-boundary canonical, module-private, projection, example, or compatibility schema.
Define reference direction and prohibit copied canonical definitions.
Define compatibility/versioning checks.
acceptance: Every schema file has exactly one canonical owner.
Duplicate canonical schema definitions equal zero.
Module-local schemas cannot silently override global contracts.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop this task and preserve evidence if any acceptance condition cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Define global .state contract
actions: Define global .state contract
Classify each state artifact as committed-generated, ignored-runtime, or prohibited.
Define regeneration, cleanup, and validation behavior.
Resolve whether any path index is permitted and ensure it does not become a second identity authority.
acceptance: Committed state is deterministic.
Runtime state is ignored and reproducible.
No permanent folder registry conflicts with the ratified identity authority.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop this task and preserve evidence if any acceptance condition cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Store migration policies as registry-backed decisions and generated projections
actions: Store approved naming, container, profile, root-artifact, global-root, schema-placement, packaging, state, and integration policies in decision, configuration, module, artifact, contract, and reuse registry records as appropriate.
Generate human-readable and compatibility policy files from those records.
Classify source naming plans and standalone policy proposals as superseded or immutable evidence.
acceptance: No standalone policy file independently owns facts already in registries.
All policy projections are deterministic and protected.
tasks.fail_closed: Parallel policy authority blocks path mapping.

pass_condition: Every module has evidence-backed profile/container/root-artifact rules

source_value_snapshot.purpose: Create a complete one-record-per-path destination matrix, resolve multi-owner/unmapped/global/generated/runtime paths, and freeze approved dispositions.

tasks.title: Generate initial file-destination matrix
actions: Generate initial file-destination matrix
Join the tracked-file baseline with file_module_mapping.csv, module manifests, service references, test references, packaging metadata, and global-root policy.
Emit unresolved records rather than guessing.
acceptance: Every tracked source path appears exactly once as a source record.
No multi-owner file is silently assigned to one module.
No unmapped path is omitted.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop this task and preserve evidence if any acceptance condition cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Resolve multi-module service files
actions: Resolve multi-module service files
Review calendar-ingestor files jointly mapped to D2, D3, F2, and D4.
Review reentry-engine files jointly mapped to E1, E2, and E4.
Review transport-router files jointly mapped to R2, O1, and B1.
For each file, approve move, split, shared compatibility layer, wrapper, or archive.
acceptance: Every multi-owner file has one approved disposition.
Every split has a split specification and behavioral equivalence test.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop this task and preserve evidence if any acceptance condition cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Resolve unmapped shared and UI files
actions: Resolve unmapped shared and UI files

Assign a module, retain globally, create a compatibility location, archive, or leave needs_review with an owner and deadline.
acceptance: Unmapped tracked paths equal zero before physical migration.
Every globally retained implementation file has a justified global owner.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop this task and preserve evidence if any acceptance condition cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Finalize destination and split specifications
actions: Finalize destination and split specifications

actions: Implement structural validator

Validate identity, locator, root name, .module-id, manifest filename, container inventory, profile structure, root artifacts, global roots, schema ownership, destination completeness, and duplicate paths.
Add report-only CI workflow.
acceptance: Validator can run on the pre-migration and proposed-tree states.
All findings have stable rule IDs and machine-readable output.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop this task and preserve evidence if any acceptance condition cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Implement migration renderers
actions: Generate expected module roots, first-level containers, nested profiles, root artifacts, global roots, and target paths from registries.
Use stable sort and LF UTF-8.
Reject collisions and path-length hazards.
acceptance: Same registry inputs produce byte-identical outputs.
No hand-authored target path enters output.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop the task and preserve evidence if acceptance cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Generate authoritative migration projections
actions: Generate target tree, rename map, rollback map, legacy path index, path impact report, global root inventory, shared-file resolution report, and validation report.
acceptance: All generated artifacts are covered by --check.
Proposed hand-authored tree is replaced as implementation input.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop the task and preserve evidence if acceptance cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Implement structural and migration validator
actions: Validate 34 roots, unique IDs/locators/symbols, .module-id, manifest identity, containers, profiles, root artifacts, path coverage, global roots, schema authority, rollback entries, stale paths, and generated parity.
Support pre-migration, per-wave, and final modes.
acceptance: All rules in validation_catalog are executable.
Mutation tests fail on each rule violation.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop the task and preserve evidence if acceptance cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Run report-only dry run without physical moves
actions: Render all operations.
Apply them in a disposable worktree or simulated filesystem.
Run path-consumer rewrites and validation.
Execute rollback.
acceptance: No default working-tree changes.
Forward and rollback simulations pass.
Unexpected path changes = 0.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop the task and preserve evidence if acceptance cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Replace the hand-authored proposed tree with generated migration projections

source_refs.filename: EAFIX Module Folder Naming and Migration Plan.txt

actions: Generate the module tree from ratified module identities, locators, container inventories, profiles, root-artifact policy, global-root policy, packaging policy, and the approved destination matrix.
Treat the proposed tree as a visual seed only.
Generate rename, impact, rollback, validation, and input-snapshot artifacts from the same pure renderer.
acceptance: Same approved inputs produce byte-identical outputs.
The generated tree contains no unsupported empty folders.
The hand-authored proposed tree is no longer authoritative.
tasks.fail_closed: Any drift between generated projections or their approved inputs blocks pilots.

tasks.title: Run the complete report-only migration dry run

source_refs.filename: EAFIX Module Folder Naming and Migration Plan.txt

actions: Run all structure, collision, path-length, naming, root-marker, packaging, import, test-discovery, deployment, MT4-path, documentation-link, generated-file, and rollback validators without moving files.
Require every path consumer to have an approved update or exception.
Produce per-wave expected diffs and verification commands.
acceptance: Physical moves performed = 0.
Unresolved path consumers = 0.
Target collisions = 0.
Rollback coverage = 100%.
tasks.fail_closed: No pilot may begin while the report-only dry run is not clean.

exit_gate.on_fail: Stop pilots and correct policy, mapping, or tooling.

source_value_snapshot.purpose: Prove the system from a clean clone, make enforcement blocking, wire real downstream consumption, publish the runbook, complete supersession, and retire the implementation plan.

tasks.title: Run clean-clone verification
actions: Run clean-clone verification
Clone the migrated branch into a clean environment.
Regenerate all generated artifacts.
Run structural validation, all applicable tests, builds, packaging checks, docs-link checks, workflows, Docker checks, PowerShell checks, and MT4 checks.

actions: Make candidate integrity, authority coverage, generated parity, structure, path coverage, and stale-path checks blocking.

acceptance: Required checks are protected and execute on master/develop or current approved branches.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop the task and preserve evidence if acceptance cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Publish completion and source-plan supersession record
actions: Record final hashes, PRs, checks, generated artifacts, authority owners, migration results, rollback status, known residuals, and archived source plans.
Update project knowledge summary/router.
acceptance: Source plans no longer appear active.
Final authority and routing are discoverable.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop the task and preserve evidence if acceptance cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Wire the first downstream consumers
actions: A correct registry nobody reads is not finished. Convert at least the first consumers from hand-authored to registry-derived: (a) generated architecture docs replace their hand-written counterparts, (b) CI derives the module dependency graph for build ordering / impact analysis from module_registry, (c) AI agent context packs (routing instructions, module briefs) are generated from registry data instead of maintained by hand, (d) implementation contracts for new work are instantiated from contract_registry records.
outputs: At least one CI job that consumes registry .current.json snapshots as input
Generated agent-context artifacts with a build-manifest provenance chain
acceptance: Deleting a registry record visibly breaks a downstream consumer's CI — proof of real dependency
tasks.fail_closed: Stop this task and preserve evidence if any acceptance condition cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Establish the change workflow and runbook
actions: Document and enforce the steady-state lifecycle: how a record is added (authored JSONL edit -> schema/FK validation -> regeneration -> PR with generated diff reviewed together), how a record is changed, how a record is retired (status transition per the authority-state matrix, never deletion), and how a new registry is introduced (index entry, schema, adapters, baseline). Add PR templates and a CODEOWNERS rule for 01_canonical_registries/.
outputs: CODEOWNERS coverage for authored registry paths
acceptance: A new record can be added end-to-end by following the runbook alone, gated entirely by existing CI
tasks.fail_closed: Stop this task and preserve evidence if any acceptance condition cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Adopt registry-first change workflow
actions: Require proposed changes to start with a decision or domain registry update.
Regenerate all projections.
Validate impact before runtime or path changes.
Reject direct edits to generated files.
acceptance: Every accepted change has decision/provenance and generated evidence.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop the task and preserve evidence if acceptance cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Implement lifecycle operations
actions: Support add, rename, split, merge, deprecate, supersede, retire, and ownership transfer without ID reuse.
Generate crosswalks and compatibility projections.
acceptance: All lifecycle transitions schema-valid and reversible where required.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop the task and preserve evidence if acceptance cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Implement scheduled drift and routing validation
actions: Regularly check authority paths, missing sources, generated parity, orphan records, stale links, and repository structure.
Notify only on meaningful failures.
acceptance: Drift is detected before release or merge.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop the task and preserve evidence if acceptance cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Define periodic governance review
actions: Review needs_review items, approved exceptions, external authority freshness, compatibility shims, retired paths, and schema versions.
Create focused issues for unresolved work.
acceptance: No temporary exception becomes permanent without approval.
tasks.fail_closed: Stop the task and preserve evidence if acceptance cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Final supersession sweep and declaration of done
actions: Audit every legacy architecture/contract/process document against doc_authority.json: each is either (a) superseded with pointer, (b) still authoritative for facts no registry covers (recorded), or (c) archived with a decision record. Publish a final system status report generated from registry data itself, and record the completion in decision_registry.
acceptance: definition_of_finished criteria all satisfied; completion decision recorded
tasks.fail_closed: Stop this task and preserve evidence if any acceptance condition cannot be proven.

tasks.title: Declare the merged program complete only from generated evidence
preconditions: All final validators and downstream consumer checks pass.
actions: Generate the final system-status report from canonical registries, authority manifest, build manifest, process projections, migration reports, and CI evidence.
Record completion in decision_registry.
Supersede this implementation plan as completed history while retaining the operations runbook and registry governance policies as active.
Confirm every predecessor plan and legacy document is classified as superseded, retained authority, retained runbook, archived history, or generated projection.
acceptance: Every definition-of-done criterion is machine-verifiable or carries approved human evidence.
Unclassified legacy artifacts = 0.
The system remains operable after the implementation plan is retired.
tasks.fail_closed: Do not declare completion from prose assertions or incomplete CI evidence.

pass_condition: At least one CI consumer depends on canonical registry data
exit_gate.on_fail: Do not declare completion; preserve the implementation plan as active until all failures close.
